title: ipomoea parasitica
intro: Ipomoea parasitica, also known as Strangle Tangle, is a perennial vine native to the tropical regions of Asia, and is classified as a parasitic plant that derives its nutrients from the host plant it infects. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with Ipomoea parasitica include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often dried and used in teas or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
